title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: A professional is decorating our living room at the moment.
‘You should take up a hobby,’ the doctor told me.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verbs with object + infinitive.

questions.firstSentence: I didn't go to the party and now I regret it.
questions.explanation: Tests 'wish' + past perfect to express past regret.

questions.firstSentence: It was impossible for Sarah to forget her keys at the office.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for negative deduction (can't have + past participle).

questions.firstSentence: I have never seen such a beautiful sunrise before.
The teacher said we didn't need to finish the essay until Monday.
questions.afterGap: to finish the essay until Monday.
questions.explanation: Tests adjective patterns and formal structures of necessity.
